name: ruby
title: ruby
summary: Interpreter of object-oriented scripting language Ruby (default version)
description:
Interpreter of object-oriented scripting language Ruby (default version)
Ruby is the interpreted scripting language for quick and easy
object-oriented programming.  It has many features to process text
files and to do system management tasks (as in perl).  It is simple,
straight-forward, and extensible.
.
This package is a dependency package, which depends on Debian's default Ruby
version (currently v3.3).

commands.name: rdoc
commands.description: Generate documentation from Ruby script files

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use erb because Interpreter of object-oriented scripting language Ruby (default version).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use erb as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `erb [--version] [-UPdnvx] [-E ext[:int]] [-S level] [-T mode] [-r library] [--] [file ...]` as documented upstream for erb.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair erb with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `erb --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install ruby-defaults (1:3.3).
